message:
viz: cluster UI panel, clustering/search tests (bd-24q.14.3/14.4/9.4 fix)

- Cluster Explorer panel: threshold/limit selectors, cluster list with
  theme tags and medoid info, select to highlight on timeline, prev/next
  in-cluster navigation buttons
- Clustering unit tests (~40 assertions): MinHash export/hydrate,
  determinism, Jaccard accuracy, grouping stability, threshold
  monotonicity, member uniqueness, sorting, theme tags, medoid validity
- Timeline cluster highlight CSS (indigo outline)
- Fix duplicate analytics function insertion from previous commit

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-24q.14","title":"Viz: Commit Clustering by Similarity (MinHash + Themes)","description":"Goal
- Group commits by similarity of their changes so users can see recurring "themes" (e.g., lots of scrivening, refactors, alien-math additions) and jump between similar edits quickly.

UX (Desktop)
- Cluster panel: list clusters with size + representative commit; clicking a cluster highlights its members on the timeline and filters the commit list.
- Optional "theme tags" derived from dominant buckets / keywords in diffs.

UX (Mobile)
- Cluster list in a sheet; selecting highlights and offers "next/prev in cluster" navigation.

Implementation Notes (Alien-Artifact Leaning)
- Use MinHash signatures over token shingles of the unified diff (or added-lines-only) to approximate Jaccard similarity efficiently.
- Run in WebWorker; cache signatures/clusters by dataset hash + params (shingle size, signature length, threshold).
- Keep clustering deterministic (stable ordering) so permalinks remain meaningful.

Acceptance Criteria
- Clusters are stable and useful; selecting a cluster makes it easy to explore similar commits in a few clicks.

Testing
- Unit tests on synthetic diffs with known overlap; assert clustering groups correctly.
- E2E: select cluster, verify timeline highlights and next/prev navigation works.","notes":"Definition of done (plan-level gate):
- All child implementation subtasks are complete.
- Child unit + e2e beads are implemented and passing with structured logs/artifacts.
- UX validated on desktop and mobile with no critical regressions in navigation or readability.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T00:48:58.584087667Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T01:17:09.283914175Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["spec","viz"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-24q.14","depends_on_id":"bd-24q","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T00:48:58.584087667Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-24q.14","depends_on_id":"bd-24q.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T00:58:32.506418433Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-24q.14","depends_on_id":"bd-24q.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T00:58:32.416387572Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-24q.15","title":"Viz: Side-by-Side Markdown Panes (A vs B) + Synced Scroll","description":"Goal
- In A/B compare mode, add a side-by-side rendered markdown view (A on left, B on right) with synced scrolling and clear change cues. This is the fastest way to grok "what changed" while keeping the spec readable.

UX (Desktop)
- Two panes with a draggable divider; optional "sync scroll" toggle (on by default).
- Sync by headings/anchors when possible; fallback to proportional scroll.
- Hovering a heading path highlights the corresponding section in both panes.

UX (Mobile)
- Stacked/segmented control: A | B | Split (if landscape).
- Sync via "jump to same heading" rather than continuous scroll.

Implementation Notes
- Reuse reconstructed snapshots from A/B compare; avoid double recompute.
- Provide a robust mapping between A and B headings (by normalized heading text + path).

Acceptance Criteria
- Side-by-side mode is usable on large docs without jank.
- Heading jumps land at consistent corresponding sections.

Testing
- Unit tests for heading matching + scroll sync mapping.
- E2E: enable A/B, switch to side-by-side, scroll in A -> B follows; mobile A/B toggle works.","notes":"Definition of done (plan-level gate):
- All child implementation subtasks are complete.
- Child unit + e2e beads are implemented and passing with structured logs/artifacts.
- UX validated on desktop and mobile with no critical regressions in navigation or readability.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T00:49:34.505264248Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T01:17:09.370621492Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["spec","viz"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-24q.15","depends_on_id":"bd-24q","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T00:49:34.505264248Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-24q.15","depends_on_id":"bd-24q.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T00:58:26.858462088Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-24q.16","title":"Viz: Inline Changed-Line Highlights in Rendered Markdown","description":"Goal
- Let users read the spec as a document *and still see what changed* inline: highlight added/changed lines/blocks directly in the rendered markdown pane (not only in Diff2Html).

UX (Desktop)
- Toggle: "Inline highlights". Added lines get a subtle left bar + background; modified blocks get a faint outline; hovering shows the bucket mix + Δ metrics for that block.
- Works with section summary and heading mini-map: changed headings show markers; clicking a marker scrolls to the next changed block.

UX (Mobile)
- Same toggle in sheet; changed blocks are navigable via "Next change" button (big tap target).

Implementation Notes
- Need a stable mapping from unified diff hunks to rendered DOM blocks. Likely approach: inject sentinel markers into the markdown before rendering (line/block wrappers), then post-process DOM to apply highlights and remove sentinels.
- Must not break code highlighting or typography.

Acceptance Criteria
- Inline highlights are accurate enough to be trusted; toggling is instant.

Testing
- Unit tests for hunk->block mapping and highlight application (synthetic markdown + diffs).
- E2E: toggle highlights, navigate next change, verify highlight present and stable across commit switches.","notes":"Definition of done (plan-level gate):
- All child implementation subtasks are complete.
- Child unit + e2e beads are implemented and passing with structured logs/artifacts.
- UX validated on desktop and mobile with no critical regressions in navigation or readability.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T00:50:26.951490454Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T01:17:09.455524746Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["spec","viz"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-24q.16","depends_on_id":"bd-24q","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T00:50:26.951490454Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-24q.16","depends_on_id":"bd-24q.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T00:58:33.225830437Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-24q.16","depends_on_id":"bd-24q.8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T00:58:33.134420007Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
